channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Rahm pays fine to return to DP World Tour as LIV doubt grows
item.description: Jon Rahm will be eligible for Europe in next year's Ryder Cup after agreeing to pay fines and play events on the DP World Tour this season, a move that also triggered speculation about his future with LIV Golf beyond 2026.

media:group.media:description: Jon Rahm has agreed to pay his fines to the DP World Tour to receive re-entry.

item.title: Arson squad probes Chery car dealership fire in Melbourne
item.description: A fire at a Port Melbourne car dealership in the early hours of the morning is being treated as suspicious.

media:group.media:description: Emergency services were called just before 5am.

item.title: Inside the historic home at the centre of Supreme Court battle
item.description: The resident trying to take adverse possession of artist Frederick McCubbin's historic former home says he wants to bring in the "right people" to fix it up.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: Trevor Hoare has filed for adverse possession of Fontainebleau on Mount Macedon.

item.title: Arsenal close in on historic double by reaching Champions League final
item.description: Arsenal reach the Champions League final for the second time in their history with a 1-0 home win over Atletico Madrid sealing a 2-1 aggregate victory in the semi-final.

media:group.media:description: Arsenal scraped a 1-0 win over Atletico Madrid to reach the Champions League final.

item.title: Witness describes frantic rescue attempt during deadly boating tragedy
item.description: Darragh Hunt was fishing at Ballina when the incident that claimed three lives unfolded before his eyes.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ross, Lachie McLeod, and Bruce MacKenzie

media:group.media:description: Two volunteers died when this NSW Marine Rescue boat capsized at Ballina.

item.title: Live: ASX to rise following gains on Wall St
item.description: The ASX is set to open higher following fresh records on two of Wall Street's major stock exchanges. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Lin Lin and Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in December 2022.

item.title: Will Donald Trump's 'Project Freedom' restart shipping or reignite war?
item.description: The US president is pledging to secure shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. But "Project Freedom" is a risky exercise that threatens to shatter a shaky ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan and Basel Hindeleh

media:group.media:description: A patrol boat moves through the water as cargo ships sit at anchor in the Strait of Hormuz, off Bandar Abbas, Iran.

item.title: Fuel price rises in Australia slow as costs soar in the US. Here's why
item.description: Fuel prices in Australia have started to moderate just as costs have reached an almost four-year high in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ahmed Yussuf

media:group.media:description: Fuel prices in Australia have started to moderate but have reached an almost four-year high in the United States.

item.title: Gibson shrugs off bagel to win through in Rome
item.description: Talia Gibson has made a fighting start on her debut on the clay of Rome, the Australian number two beating former world top-20 player Martina Trevisan to progress.

media:group.media:description: Talia Gibson lost the second set to love but recovered to beat Martina Trevisan.

item.title: Live: Chalmers says fuel security package to be unveiled following National Cabinet
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says a fuel security package will be unveiled following today's meeting of National Cabinet. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor gave a speech and took part in a Q&A at a Chamber of Minerals and Energy of WA event in Perth on Monday, April 20, 2026.

item.title: Paperwork blunder allows bowls club's 'secret sell-off' to developers
item.description: Coorparoo Bowls Club's committee says a paperwork blunder allowed Bowls Queensland to evict them from the land so they could sell it to developers.
item.dc:creator: Kenji Sato and Ellen Fanning

media:group.media:description: Mark Delbridge says the club refuses to go without a fight.

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family feel let down after missing court hearing
item.description: The family of a five-year-old girl who died after going missing from an Alice Springs town camp last month say they feel let down by the system after missing her alleged killer's first court date.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange and Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: Robin Japanangka Granites says prosecutors did not speak with Kumanjayi's family to inform them of how the case would proceed.

item.title: Nidhi fought for 10 years to get on the NDIS. The new cuts have left her 'in limbo'
item.description: Nidhi Shekaran is one of thousands of NDIS participants waiting to learn if their services will be impacted as part of sweeping government reforms to the scheme.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Viñales

media:group.media:description: NDIS recipient Nidhi Shekaran says the new cuts are "anxiety-driven".

item.title: Medicines regulator urged to recall controversial cold and flu herb
item.description: Andrographis paniculata is a common ingredient in cold and flu supplements, but it may not be as low-risk as first thought.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Woodburn

media:group.media:description: James De Bond says he was advised by a doctor not to take products containing Andrographis paniculata again.

item.title: Negative gearing, capital gains tax and trusts explained simply
item.description: The government is poised to make changes to negative gearing, the capital gains tax discount, and the tax treatment of trusts in next week's federal budget. So how do they work, and what could the changes look like?
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Labor is set to make changes to negative gearing, the capital gains tax, and trusts in the federal budget.

item.title: Big new hotel announced for Adelaide but are there enough already?
item.description: The latest major hotel project announced for Adelaide adds to a looming accommodation construction boom in the CBD.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Holder and Eugene Boisvert

media:group.media:description: A composite image of the Crystalbrook Sam, Hilton East End Adelaide and Adelaide Marriott hotels.

item.title: Save money on groceries with 'relatively little effort'
item.description: With grocery prices continuing to bite, more Australians are discovering that home gardening isn't just good for wellbeing — it can also make real financial sense.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Breen and Bec Pridham

media:group.media:description: Graphic of a no-dig garden cross-section

item.title: EV tax discount removed two days' worth of carbon emissions
item.description: Two days' worth of carbon emissions were removed thanks to the $2 billion electric vehicle tax discount. But its supporters say that put 64,000 more EVs on the road, jump-starting the struggling industry.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: The EV tax discount abated two days' worth of emissions, a Treasury review has concluded.

item.title: Calls to commemorate secret island training camp for elite WWII soldiers
item.description: Residents call for the creation of a permanent memorial for a secret military school on K'gari (Fraser Island) that trained elite soldiers during World War II.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Nunes-Malek and James C Taylor

media:group.media:description: Elite commandos were trained at a secret base on K'gari during World War II.

item.title: Man charged with manslaughter, drug driving over Woodford crash
item.description: Police charge a 31-year-old Brisbane man with manslaughter following a fatal crash allegedly involving a stolen utility at Woodford.
item.dc:creator: Madeline Grace

media:group.media:description: The driver has been charged with failling to remain at the scene.

item.title: 'Gut-wrenching' struggle for Australian designers finding copies on Shein
item.description: Small Australian fashion designers say it is too time-consuming to continue to ask Shein to take down copies of their designs.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: Things You Really Like designer Klaudia Burzynska found her design being sold on Shein.

item.title: Live: Rubio says 'Operation Epic Fury' concluded but with conditions
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has defended itself against more strikes, as the US tries to clear safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Iran says it is preventing unauthorised vessels from transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Phone books are still being delivered but does anyone use them?
item.description: Once a must-have household item, are phone books destined to go the way of the answering machine and rotary dial phone?
item.dc:creator: Coco Veldkamp and Jeremy Lee

media:group.media:description: The first Yellow Pages directory in Australia was published in the early 1970s.

item.title: Three men walked into a whisky bar. Eight years on, a court case has ended
item.description: The Salamanca Whisky Bar was accused of selling fake versions of the award-wining Sullivans Cove whisky. But eight years after the saga began, a court in Hobart has dismissed the charges.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Sullivans Cove Distillery became the first distillery outside of Scotland or Japan to win the world's best single malt at the World Whiskies Awards.

item.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia's 'utter cynicism' as strikes kill 22 before ceasefire
item.description: The Ukrainian president called one attack in Poltava "especially vile", accusing Russia of launching a second missile while emergency rescuers were at the scene.

media:group.media:description: The aftermath of a Russian air strike in Zaporizhzhia.
